title: Phyllocactus nelsonii
taxonomy: Phyllocactus nelsonii is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Cactaceae. It belongs to the genus Phyllocactus, which is a group of epiphytic cacti. The plant is native to Mexico and is known for its long, hanging stems and funnel-shaped flowers. Phyllocactus nelsonii is a type of epiphytic cactus that grows in the wild, often attached to trees. Its classification reflects its unique growth habits and adaptations to its native environment.
